Expectation & Reconstruction
›
1Prior Expectation
What did you expect from this wine, producer, or region before the encounter?
Guided expectation profile
2Contradictory Reality
What did you actually find — and how did it diverge from your expectation?
Main divergence driver
3Reconstruction Attempt
What did you do to understand the divergence? Reread notes, researched, discussed, compared, etc.
Primary action taken
4Revised Understanding
What do you now believe about this wine, producer, region, or framework?
What changed most?
5Comparison Marker
How does this change your prior model? What specifically shifts — and how much?
Old: [prior understanding]
New: [revised understanding]
Shift level
6Confidence In The Revision
How solid does the new understanding feel?
1 = tentative3 = moderate5 = solid
Blind Tasting Assistant
Observe → Interpret → Commit → Reveal
Checkpoint-based deduction. No grape or region suggestions appear until the palate is locked.
1. Setup
Define the boundaries without revealing identity.
2. Appearance
Record what is visible. Do not infer yet.
Still no candidate suggestions after this stage.
3A. Nose — still glass
Capture volatile first impressions before aeration changes the profile.
3B. Nose — after swirling
Still no candidate ranking by default.
4. Palate & structure
This stage triggers the first real deduction.
5. Deduction Panel
Lock the palate to generate candidate ranking.
6. Final call
Commit before reveal. This prevents post-rationalization.
7. Reveal
Enter the actual wine identity.
8. Reconstruction
What should you recognize faster next time?
My records
—
Backups are saved as JSON files. Records remain local unless you export and store the file elsewhere.
